pages.text: It was Diwali morning in Jaipur, and <name> woke up to the smell of Amma’s hot jalebis and the crackle of diyas being placed on the balcony. The whole neighborhood shimmered with lights, and children were already running around with sparklers shouting, “Happy Diwali!”

<name> helped Papa hang paper lanterns, but when she picked up one of the small clay diyas, she noticed a folded note inside. “Huh? What’s this?” she whispered. The note read: “Find the place where light meets song — your next clue waits where the parrots belong!”

Her eyes sparkled. “A Diwali treasure hunt!” She ran to the garden where her pet parrot, Mithu, chirped loudly. “Kaa-kaw! Look under the swing!” Mithu squawked. <name> giggled and peeked beneath the swing. There lay another note wrapped around a tiny ladoo.

The note read: “You’re doing great, little star! The next clue shines near Papa’s car.” Racing outside, she spotted a sparkling rangoli beside the car’s tire. Beneath a flower petal, another message waited. “Go to the terrace — the sky is your guide!”
When she reached the terrace, fairy lights blinked like tiny stars. Amma, Papa, and her best friend Aarav stood there with a glowing lantern and a big smile. “Surprise!” they shouted.
Papa handed her a small box. Inside was a bracelet with a star-shaped charm. “You solved all the clues!” said Amma. “This is for our brightest Diwali light.”

<name> hugged them all. “This was the best adventure ever!” she said. That night, as fireworks painted the sky in pinks and golds, <name> whispered to Mithu, “Diwali isn’t just about lights — it’s about the love that shines in our hearts.”
Mithu chirped happily, “Twinkle-twinkle!” and the night sparkled even more brightly.
